era_context: Early Iron Age II. Shalmaneser III continues his reign and military campaigns. The Western Zhou dynasty in China is in its final years. Greek city-states are developing their political and cultural institutions. Egypt remains fragmented under competing power centers.

events.title: Shalmaneser III's military campaigns continue
events.description: Shalmaneser III's military campaigns continue as part of his broader program of Assyrian expansion and consolidation. These campaigns are documented in royal inscriptions and reliefs from his palace at Kalhu.

sources.name: Shalmaneser III's royal inscriptions and palace reliefs from Kalhu

events.certainty_note: Shalmaneser III's reign is well-attested (858–824 BCE). Specific annual attribution is approximate.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are documented for 796 BCE. The entry reflects the continuation of known patterns rather than a documented event.
historiographic_note: 796 BCE is not marked by any specific documented event. The historical record is sparse and relies on inference from the broader pattern of Shalmaneser III's reign.

graph_edges.note: Continuation of Shalmaneser III's military program
